findings.title: Composition: client-supplied fingerprint is trusted verbatim for merge/increment decisions, letting any same-origin script forge occurrence counts on an arbitrary existing report
findings.why_it_matters: computeFingerprint() runs entirely client-side (webui_app/static/js/lib/error-capture-core.js) and the resulting string is sent as a plain fingerprint field in the POST body; create_error_report() takes sanitized.get("fingerprint") as-is and calls error_report_store.find_by_fingerprint(fingerprint) -> increment_occurrence(existing_id) with no server-side recomputation from message/stack/source to verify the fingerprint actually describes the submitted error. tests/test_webui_api_v1_error_reports.py demonstrates this directly (_auto_report(fingerprint="fp-dup", reportId="corr-2") freely picks an arbitrary literal string). Any same-origin script capable of a CSRF-token-bearing POST (a compromised npm dependency in the SPA bundle, a malicious browser extension content script, or a future XSS) can first GET /api/v1/error-reports to read existing rows' fingerprint values, then repeatedly POST a body with an observed fingerprint to inflate occurrences/last_seen_at on any open report it chooses, or conversely mask a real recurring bug's fingerprint so its own genuine future recurrences get silently folded away. This corrupts the operator dashboard's triage signal without the underlying bug ever needing to actually reoccur.
evidence: 1. Attacker script runs same-origin (compromised dependency / extension / future XSS) with a valid CSRF token already available in the page.
2. GET /api/v1/error-reports returns items including each row's fingerprint field (webui_app/api/v1/error_reports.py list_error_reports).
3. Attacker POSTs a body like reportId: x, fingerprint: observed-fingerprint, message: noise to /api/v1/error-reports.

4. create_error_report() computes fingerprint = sanitized.get("fingerprint") (the attacker's raw string, unchanged by sanitize_error_report which only scrubs for secrets/length, never recomputes it) and calls find_by_fingerprint(fingerprint) -> increment_occurrence(existing_id), bumping the target row's occurrence count and last_seen_at with content that has no relationship to the row's actual error.

findings.title: Cascade: an uncaught Pinia action rejection double-submits as two distinct error-report rows because the context-prefixed message changes the fingerprint
findings.why_it_matters: The module's own comment cites vuejs/pinia#576: a store action rejection sometimes does NOT bubble to window.unhandledrejection, which implies it sometimes DOES. When a component calls a Pinia action without catching its rejection, the same Error instance can be delivered to both hook 5 ($onAction onError -> reportPiniaActionError) and hook 1 (window unhandledrejection -> onUnhandledRejection). reportPiniaActionError/reportMutationError/reportQueryError all call withContextPrefix(), which prepends a storeId.actionName-style prefix to errorInfo.message before computeFingerprint() runs; the raw window listener does not add this prefix. Since computeFingerprint() hashes name+message+first stack frames (not source), the two captures of the identical failure produce different fingerprints and are therefore each treated as a first occurrence by the per-tab DedupTracker and each POSTed as a genuinely new row server-side (no fingerprint match, so no merge). One real failure becomes two dashboard rows and consumes two units of the daily new-report cap.
evidence: 1. A Pinia store action throws/rejects and the calling component code does not catch it.

2. Pinia's $onAction onError fires (errorCapturePlugin.ts) -> reportPiniaActionError(storeId, actionName, error) -> withContextPrefix() prepends the store/action context to the message -> computeFingerprint(prefixed info) -> submitted as row A.

3. The same rejection, uncaught by the caller, also reaches window unhandledrejection -> onUnhandledRejection captures the same Error object with its raw (unprefixed) message -> computeFingerprint(raw info) produces a different fingerprint (message text differs even though name/stack match) -> submitted as row B.

4. Server-side find_by_fingerprint() sees two different fingerprint values, so both POSTs insert fresh rows via add() rather than merging via increment_occurrence -- one real error now appears as two independent, unlinked reports in the operator dashboard, and both count against _enforce_daily_cap's per-day new-row quota.

findings.title: Assumption violation: Layer-3 exact-secret masking has no minimum-length floor, so an operator-configured short credential over-redacts ordinary substrings in every report field
findings.why_it_matters: _mask_known_secret_values() does a plain substring replace (if secret and secret in s) with no minimum-length check, and _known_secret_values() gathers every configured credential value with only a truthiness check. None of save_devto_token/save_hackmd_token/... (src/backlink_publisher/config/tokens.py) enforce a minimum credential length, so an operator who pastes a short placeholder/dev-test token (e.g. a 3-4 char sandbox key) while testing a platform integration causes every future error report's message/stack/user_description/structured context to have every occurrence of that short substring silently replaced with the mask marker, anywhere it appears, including inside ordinary words/tokens/hex fragments that have nothing to do with the credential. Because Layer 3 runs after scrub_text and before the length cap, this can silently garble diagnostic text with no marker distinguishing a real secret found from a short credential value that happened to appear as a substring, undermining the sanitizer's own goal of preserving surrounding non-secret text -- the paired-positive tests in tests/test_error_report_sanitizer.py only cover a 13-15 char credential, never the pathological short-length boundary.
evidence: 1. Operator configures e.g. devto/hackmd/mataroa/qiita credential (or the FRW/LLM api_key) with a short string (no length validation exists in tokens.py's save_* functions or credential_service).
2. That short string becomes a member of the known_secrets frozenset returned by _known_secret_values() on every sanitize_error_report() call (no caching, re-read every time).
3. A later, unrelated frontend error's message/stack/user_description happens to contain that exact short substring (plausible for 3-6 char strings against natural-language or code-identifier text).

4. _mask_known_secret_values() replaces every occurrence with the mask marker regardless of context, silently mutating diagnostic text an operator relies on to triage the real bug, with no signal that this occurred versus a genuine secret redaction.

residual_risks: isSelfOriginatedError (webui_app/static/js/lib/error-capture-core.js) matches literal filenames like errorCapture.ts / errorCapturePlugin.ts in a stack/filename string; the module's own comment acknowledges this match is imperfect against Vite's hashed production chunk names. In production the Vue-side self-report-loop guard is therefore largely inert -- a genuine bug in the capture/submission pipeline that manages to throw outside its own try/catch (captureAndSubmit currently wraps its whole body, so this requires a future code change to actually trigger) would not be recognized as self-originated and could feed an unbounded self-report loop. No current code path was found that escapes captureAndSubmit's try/catch, so this is speculative rather than actively exploitable today.

_enforce_daily_cap()'s check-then-act (count rows, then later add()) is a non-atomic race under concurrent submissions; the code explicitly documents and accepts this as consistent with this store family's single-process concurrency posture (docs/solutions/architecture-patterns/2026-06-05-lite-accepted-deferrals.md), so it is called out here only as a residual risk, not a new finding.

DedupTracker (webui_app/static/js/lib/error-capture-core.js) is per-tab, in-memory, module-level state; opening many tabs or reloading the page trivially resets the 50-submission session cap and the 60s per-fingerprint window. The server-side fingerprint merge bounds row creation for a given fingerprint, but since increment_occurrence() has no rate limit of its own, a page that reloads or is opened in many tabs during a real recurring failure can drive an effectively unbounded rate of increment_occurrence writes to webui.db, amplified further if the fingerprint-trust gap (finding 1) is combined with automated reloading.
testing_gaps: No test exercises a POST whose client-supplied fingerprint deliberately does NOT correspond to its own message/stack/source (i.e. a forged fingerprint targeting a different, pre-existing report) -- every test in tests/test_webui_api_v1_error_reports.py picks fingerprint values that are self-consistent with the test's own scenario, never adversarially mismatched.

No test exercises the same underlying error reaching two of the five Vue capture hooks simultaneously (e.g. an uncaught Pinia action rejection also reaching window unhandledrejection), so the double-submission-via-differing-context-prefix scenario has no regression coverage in frontend/src/lib/errorCapture.spec.ts.

tests/test_error_report_sanitizer.py's known-short-credential test uses a 13-15 character value; there is no test for a credential at the pathological low end (1-4 chars) verifying either that over-redaction of ordinary text is acceptable/expected or that a minimum-length floor is enforced.
